methodology: Competitor universe assembled from (a) existing research_report.md (Apr 2026), (b) clinic websites (Raffles, Parkway Shenton, Thomson, AsiaMedic, Lifescan, Gleneagles Johor, Sunway, Pantai, KPJ), (c) healthcare aggregator sites (Health365, Medical Departures, Majikan.my), and (d) insurer wellness schemes (AIA Vitality, Prudential, Great Eastern). MYR prices converted to SGD at 3.3 MYR/SGD (per Apr 2026 FX in research_report.md, NOT 3.5). THB at 25.5 THB/SGD. Pricing flagged: 'public' = listed on-site, 'partial' = aggregator-disclosed, 'hidden_estimated' = not publicly listed, inferred from aggregator listings or verbal quotes. AEVUM reference pricing: Core RM1,999 / Comprehensive RM8,699 / Legacy Ultimate RM17,499 / Legacy Platinum RM24,499.

categories.sg_premium_clinic: Singapore premium executive health-screening brands (hospital-anchored): Raffles, Mt Elizabeth/Parkway, Thomson, Prenetics, Chi Longevity.
categories.sg_wholebody_mri: Singapore standalone whole-body MRI / advanced screening centres: AsiaMedic, Lifescan, Healthscreening.sg, Cohen, BodyMed.
categories.jb_private_hospital: Johor-based private hospitals offering health screening + MRI, including IHH and KPJ networks.
categories.jb_mri_centre: JB/Johor standalone radiology and diagnostic centres (non-hospital).
categories.kl_penang_premium: KL, Penang, Subang Jaya premium hospitals and clinics that attract JB/SG cross-border patients.
categories.regional_health_tourism: Thailand and other regional medical-tourism destinations competing for SG/ID HNW screening flow.
categories.corporate_wellness: Insurance-bundled wellness programs that subsidise or bundle MRI/health screening.
categories.public_option: Public / subsidised screening alternatives (MOH Polyclinic, Screen for Life, workplace schemes, Mount Alvernia low-cost screening).

competitors.name: Raffles Medical — Raffles Platinum Executive Health Screening
competitors.target_market: Singapore C-suite executives aged 45+ wanting half-day hospital concierge screening with endoscopy
competitors.primary_value_prop: Flagship Singapore hospital brand with endoscopy + cardiac + imaging in a single half-day concierge package.
strengths: Deep clinical credibility — 40+ year Singapore hospital brand
Full endoscopy (gastroscopy + colonoscopy) bundled, which AEVUM cannot easily replicate
Corporate accounts with most SG MNCs — captive demand pipeline
weaknesses: Does NOT include a true 60-min head-to-toe whole-body MRI — mostly labs + targeted imaging
Commoditised hospital feel, not a wellness/lifestyle brand
40% more expensive than AEVUM Legacy Platinum for arguably narrower imaging depth

competitors.name: Mount Elizabeth / Parkway Shenton — Screen Excelsior
competitors.target_market: SG HNW and senior executives aged 45+ who default to IHH/Parkway for lifelong care
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore's highest-priced hospital screening with private lounge, care manager and same-day results.
strengths: Prestige of Mount Elizabeth Orchard address — strongest SG HNW brand halo
Care-manager concierge + same-day report — best-in-class SG turnaround
IHH group means continuity with Gleneagles specialists
weaknesses: ~40% premium over AEVUM Legacy Platinum for largely overlapping scope
Whole-body MRI not the hero — imaging is targeted regions + ultrasound
No longevity/lifestyle narrative — still positioned as a medical checkup

competitors.name: Thomson Medical — Prestige Health Screening
competitors.target_market: Upper-middle-class Singaporean families aged 35-55, Singlife/Prudential policyholders
competitors.primary_value_prop: Mass-premium screening at accessible price point with Thomson Hospital clinical backing.
strengths: Strong price-value ratio — ~25% of Mt E Screen Excelsior price
Women's health specialisation (Thomson brand)
Integrated with Singlife/Pru insurance rails
weaknesses: No standalone whole-body MRI offering in tier
Brand feels 'maternity/family' not 'longevity/executive'
Limited concierge — still a standard screening centre experience

competitors.name: Prenetics (Singapore + HK)
competitors.target_market: Digitally-native SG/HK professionals aged 30-45 who buy genetic + metabolic screening online
competitors.primary_value_prop: Consumer-direct genomics and metabolic screening (CircleDNA, Insighta) with strong digital UX.
strengths: Digital-first brand and mobile app — UX reference point
NASDAQ-listed, well-funded, with ex-Alibaba Health partnerships
Strong story around early cancer detection via blood tests (Insighta partnership with HKU)
weaknesses: Does NOT offer in-house MRI — outsources imaging if needed
Consumer-kit feel clashes with HNW concierge expectations
Insighta Fragma test launching ~2026 is blood-based, not imaging

competitors.name: Chi Longevity — Four Seasons Singapore
competitors.target_market: HNW SG & visiting APAC executives aged 40+ staying at Four Seasons / seeking hotel-based longevity
competitors.primary_value_prop: First hotel-anchored longevity clinic in Asia, combining biomarkers, hyperbaric O2, and personalised coaching.
strengths: Four Seasons hotel setting — AEVUM's closest experience-design rival
Founded by Dr Andrea Maier (TU/e, U-Melbourne longevity chair) — credibility moat
First-mover in Singapore hotel-longevity category, opened Jan 2025
weaknesses: No in-house whole-body MRI — imaging outsourced
Premium pricing without imaging depth may feel thin to clinical buyers
Tiny capacity — cannot serve cross-border volume

competitors.name: AsiaMedic — Navigator 7
competitors.target_market: SG professionals aged 40-60 wanting the deepest single-day screening outside a hospital
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore's most comprehensive standalone screening centre, with 3T MRI and PET-CT onsite.
strengths: True whole-body MRI included — one of very few SG offerings to do so
Shenton Way location ideal for CBD executives
SGX-listed parent (AsiaMedic Limited) gives financial credibility
weaknesses: Dated interior and brand feel — not a 'lifestyle' clinic
Results turnaround 5-7 days, slower than AEVUM target
No concierge transport / private lounge model

competitors.name: Lifescan Medical (Novena / Orchard / City Square)
competitors.target_market: SG executives aged 40+ wanting MRI + 50-cancer multi-cancer early detection in a single day
competitors.primary_value_prop: One-day advanced screening that combines MRI imaging with Galleri multi-cancer early detection blood test.
strengths: Galleri 50-cancer MCED included in top tier — genuine technical differentiator
Three SG locations including City Square (relevant to JB cross-border)
Strong radiology partnerships for rapid reads
weaknesses: Top tier costs ~30% more than AEVUM Legacy Ultimate for similar scope
Brand awareness among JB/ID HNW is low
Less emphasis on hospitality / concierge design

competitors.name: Healthscreening.sg
competitors.target_market: Price-sensitive SG patients needing add-on MRI without the hospital premium
competitors.pricing_range_published_local: SGD 76 basic; MRI add-on from SGD 1,090
competitors.primary_value_prop: CHAS-accredited clinic offering affordable single-region MRI and customisable screening add-ons.
strengths: Lowest-priced imaging add-on in SG market
CHAS accreditation enables Medisave utilisation for some items
Builds demand-awareness for WB-MRI at mass-market level
weaknesses: Single-region MRI only — not true whole-body
No concierge or premium experience
Cannot credibly serve HNW segment — price competes the wrong direction

competitors.name: Cohen Medical Centre
competitors.target_market: SG longevity-curious professionals aged 35-55 seeking clinical-precision medicine + aesthetic bundle
competitors.primary_value_prop: Precision-medicine clinic combining health screening, aesthetics, and longevity biomarkers in one brand.
strengths: Strong positioning in longevity/aesthetics crossover space
Orchard address and affluent clientele
Recognised by Tatler/Prestige Singapore for longevity practice
weaknesses: No in-house whole-body MRI scanner — imaging referred out
Boutique scale limits corporate cross-border capacity
Pricing opaque — lacks public package structure

competitors.name: BodyMed Singapore (Body Medical)
competitors.target_market: SG executives aged 35-55 referred via GP for advanced whole-body imaging
competitors.primary_value_prop: Specialist imaging and screening clinic with whole-body MRI offered as a standalone diagnostic product.
strengths: Direct SG-based whole-body MRI alternative at moderate price
Strong radiologist panel; clinician-led model
Private clinic feel, less commoditised than hospital brands
weaknesses: Limited brand presence beyond SG medical community
No concierge transport / longevity framing
Low AEVUM-segment awareness in JB / ID markets

competitors.name: Gleneagles Hospital Johor (Medini)
competitors.target_market: SG cross-border patients and Johor HNW families preferring IHH brand continuity with SG Gleneagles
competitors.primary_value_prop: IHH-backed hospital in Medini Iskandar offering full-spectrum screening with SG brand continuity.
strengths: IHH/Gleneagles brand trust bridges SG to JB seamlessly
Medini Iskandar location close to 2nd Link and future RTS feeder
Multidisciplinary specialist bench in-house
weaknesses: No longevity / lifestyle framing — still hospital experience
Transparent MRI pricing not published (aggregator-listed only)
Long wait times reported on Google reviews during peak

competitors.name: KPJ Johor Specialist Hospital
competitors.target_market: Johor Malaysian middle-class families and SG price-sensitive patients seeking comprehensive screening
competitors.primary_value_prop: Largest Malaysian private hospital network with strong Johor presence and Medisave-approved status.
strengths: KPJ brand is top-of-mind in Malaysia — 30+ hospitals nationwide
Medisave-approved — SG patients can use Medisave/Integrated Shield
Volume keeps prices low and throughput high
weaknesses: Hospital feel; noisy and crowded relative to AEVUM concept
Imaging tech often 1.5T — not 3T standard
Weak digital/booking experience vs SG benchmarks

competitors.name: Regency Specialist Hospital (Masai, JB)
competitors.target_market: SG-facing patients at the JB eastern corridor seeking cost arbitrage on screening & specialist care
competitors.primary_value_prop: Explicitly SG-cross-border-oriented hospital near 2nd Link with direct SG marketing channels.
strengths: Geographic proximity to 2nd Link — captures driving SG traffic
Publishes SG-oriented content and shuttle packages
Medisave-approved for eligible claim types
weaknesses: Brand strength vs KPJ/Gleneagles is low
Imaging tech mostly 1.5T; no whole-body MRI product
Limited marketing to HNW segment

competitors.name: Columbia Asia Hospital — Iskandar Puteri
competitors.target_market: Medini/Puteri Harbour residents and expats in Iskandar corridor seeking mid-premium screening
competitors.primary_value_prop: Pan-Asia hospital chain in Iskandar Puteri close to EduCity/Medini expats.
strengths: Strong footprint in Iskandar Puteri catchment
English-first service standard for expat population
Clean, modern facility by JB hospital standards
weaknesses: Not marketed as premium — mid-tier general hospital feel
No whole-body MRI longevity product
Lower brand prestige than IHH or KPJ

competitors.name: Mahkota Medical Centre (Melaka)
competitors.target_market: SG/Indonesian medical tourists driving up North Malaysia corridor for multi-day treatment
competitors.primary_value_prop: Leading medical-tourism hospital in Melaka with SG/ID patient concierge desk and airport transfers.
strengths: Decades of medical-tourism operating experience with SG/ID inbound
Airport transfer + translator service already productised
Strong oncology/cardio specialty depth
weaknesses: Geographic disadvantage — 2.5hrs from JB, 3.5hrs from SG
Aging facility imagery online
Not positioned as longevity / lifestyle

competitors.name: Pantai Hospital Batu Pahat
competitors.target_market: Johor state residents in Batu Pahat/Muar seeking IHH-network screening locally
competitors.pricing_range_published_local: RM 800–1,500 (MRI single region) — no WB MRI
competitors.primary_value_prop: IHH Pantai's Batu Pahat outpost serving north-Johor residents with general-purpose imaging.
strengths: IHH brand recognition in Malaysia
Lowest MRI single-region price in Johor state
Locally dominant in Batu Pahat catchment
weaknesses: Not JB proper — 1.5hrs from AEVUM catchment
1.5T scanner tier; no whole-body MRI offering
Facility feels dated; no premium positioning

competitors.name: Smart Diagnostic Imaging (JB)
competitors.target_market: Johor GP-referred patients needing specific-region MRI at budget price
competitors.primary_value_prop: Standalone JB diagnostic centre offering cheap single-region MRI for referred patients.
strengths: One of the cheapest MRI options in JB
Fast turnaround for single-region studies
Direct GP referral model keeps costs low
weaknesses: Does not offer whole-body MRI
1.5T scanners; no AI tooling
No concierge or patient-experience layer

competitors.name: Intouch Medical Centre (JB)
competitors.target_market: Johor middle-class professionals seeking walk-in diagnostics and basic screening
competitors.primary_value_prop: GP-plus clinic in JB with imaging referral partnerships.
strengths: Low overhead, low prices for basic imaging
GP + screening hybrid model
JB-local familiarity
weaknesses: No on-site MRI — imaging referred to hospital partners
No WB-MRI / longevity offering
Minimal brand presence

competitors.name: Omega Clinic (JB)
competitors.target_market: Boutique SG-facing patients seeking doctor-led check-ups in JB without hospital feel
competitors.primary_value_prop: Small doctor-led clinic in JB catering to Singaporean cross-border patients.
strengths: Boutique, unrushed clinical experience
SG-facing marketing and bilingual staff
Closest brand-tone adjacent to AEVUM in JB today
weaknesses: No in-house MRI — must refer out
Tiny capacity, single-clinic operation
Weak digital presence and brand scale

competitors.name: Pantai Hospital Ayer Keroh / JB radiology (IHH)
competitors.target_market: Johor/Melaka IHH policyholders needing MRI imaging as part of Pantai network care
competitors.primary_value_prop: Pantai network standardised MRI pricing accessible via any IHH outpost.
strengths: IHH network consistency across Malaysia
Transparent published pricing (one of few MY hospitals)
Strong insurer panel coverage
weaknesses: Single-region MRI only
No longevity or lifestyle framing
1.5T / 3T mix depending on branch

competitors.name: Sunway Medical Centre (Sunway City, KL)
competitors.target_market: Klang Valley HNW and regional medical tourists booking integrated screening + specialist care
competitors.pricing_range_published_local: RM 1,500–3,000 per MRI; tiered wellness packages
competitors.primary_value_prop: Malaysia's leading medical-tourism hospital with 3T scanner and established SG/ID inbound flow.
strengths: Decades of medical-tourism operations and inbound concierge
Transparent pricing on website (rare for MY hospitals)
3T scanner, strong oncology/cardio depth
weaknesses: 300km from JB — not in AEVUM's primary catchment
Hospital environment, not lifestyle/longevity brand
No whole-body MRI as branded product

competitors.name: Prince Court Medical Centre (KL)
competitors.target_market: KL HNW, diplomatic and royal-linked patients seeking the highest-prestige MY hospital
competitors.primary_value_prop: Malaysia's most prestigious private hospital (owned by IHH), frequently ranked top in medical-tourism awards.
strengths: Highest brand prestige in Malaysia; royal-associated heritage
Strong oncology/cardio subspecialty credentials
English-first, diplomatic-ready patient services
weaknesses: KL location — not JB / not AEVUM adjacent
Pricing opaque; no published packages
Hospital ambience, no longevity branding

competitors.name: Subang Jaya Medical Centre (SJMC / Ramsay Sime Darby)
competitors.target_market: Klang Valley corporate and family patients seeking mid-premium screening and heart care
competitors.pricing_range_published_local: RM 794 year-end promo package; RM 3,000 Heart Screen
competitors.primary_value_prop: Ramsay Sime Darby flagship with aggressive promotional pricing on screening packages.
strengths: Best price-promoter in KL — frequent RM 800 entry packages
Strong cardiac specialty anchor
Ramsay group financial stability
weaknesses: Not JB adjacent
Promo pricing undermines premium positioning
No standalone whole-body MRI branded offer

competitors.name: BP Healthcare Group (KL + JB)
competitors.target_market: Malaysian mass-premium retail consumers buying bundled screening via retail storefronts
competitors.primary_value_prop: Retail-clinic healthcare chain with aggressive bundle pricing and B2B corporate wellness contracts.
strengths: Retail footprint across Malaysia including JB
Brand recognition among mass-premium Malaysians
Corporate wellness contracts captive customer base
weaknesses: Retail / mall-clinic feel — incompatible with AEVUM HNW positioning
Imaging is lab-partner model, not in-house 3T
Does not offer true whole-body MRI

competitors.name: Bumrungrad International Hospital
competitors.target_market: Affluent SG/ID/ME patients flying to Bangkok for multi-day screening + lifestyle trip
competitors.primary_value_prop: Thailand's flagship medical-tourism hospital with full concierge and translator services in 30+ languages.
strengths: Global brand, 1M+ international patients/year
Full concierge including airport, hotel bundles
Deep specialty benches far beyond screening
weaknesses: Requires a flight and overnight — higher friction than JB
Hospital environment, not longevity-branded
Limited standalone whole-body MRI product

competitors.name: Bangkok Hospital (BDMS)
competitors.target_market: SG/ID/ME medical tourists preferring BDMS network hospitals across Thailand
competitors.primary_value_prop: BDMS network flagship with large international-patient infrastructure and tiered health packages.
strengths: Largest private hospital network in Thailand
Medical tourism machine — airport transfer, translator, hotel included
Strong cardiac and orthopaedic specialties
weaknesses: Flight + overnight friction
No branded whole-body MRI longevity product
Hospital feel, not lifestyle concierge

competitors.name: Thonburi Hospital / Thonburi Healthcare Group
competitors.target_market: Regional ME/CIS/ASEAN patients seeking mid-premium medical tourism in Bangkok
competitors.primary_value_prop: Thonburi group's multi-hospital brand with strong inbound patient flow from Middle East and Myanmar.
strengths: Lower price than Bumrungrad / Bangkok Hospital
Multi-hospital network
Strong renal and cardiac specialty volume
weaknesses: Less known among SG HNW
Facility aesthetics below AEVUM target
No longevity framing

competitors.name: RAKxa Wellness (Bangkok)
competitors.target_market: APAC HNW wellness tourists seeking multi-day medical + spa retreats combining screening and therapy
competitors.primary_value_prop: Asia's most advanced medical-wellness retreat, combining functional medicine, biomarkers and imaging in a destination setting.
strengths: Closest Asian analogue to Chenot/SHA — multi-day destination model
Full-spectrum spa + functional medicine + imaging
High-touch hospitality far above hospital brands
weaknesses: Requires flight + 3-5 nights — high-friction alternative
Multi-day commitment filters out working executives
Pricing > AEVUM Legacy Platinum

competitors.name: AIA Vitality Clinic (SG) / AIA Vitality programme
competitors.target_market: AIA life/health policyholders earning Vitality points to subsidise annual screening
competitors.pricing_range_published_local: Bundled with AIA policies; SGD 150–800 screening vouchers
competitors.primary_value_prop: Insurer-subsidised health screening programme that rewards policyholders for preventive care.
strengths: Captive customer base of AIA policyholders in SG and MY
Point-based behavioural incentives drive annual screening habit
Partnership with Thomson/Parkway clinics for redemption
weaknesses: Does NOT include whole-body MRI — screenings are basic/mid-tier
Not a standalone clinic brand
Vitality points can't be used for premium AEVUM-tier scans

competitors.name: Prudential PRUActive / PRUShield Wellness
competitors.target_market: Prudential policyholders in SG/MY with Integrated Shield plans seeking bundled screening
competitors.primary_value_prop: Prudential wellness programme subsidising annual screening and promoting preventive habits.
strengths: Large SG/MY policyholder base
Strong clinic partnership network
Integrated Shield riders fund specialist follow-up
weaknesses: No whole-body MRI coverage
Premium HNW segment typically bypasses bundle for direct-pay screening
Programme is insurance-loss-ratio driven, not experience-driven

competitors.name: Great Eastern Live Great Programme
competitors.target_market: Great Eastern policyholders and OCBC Premier Banking clients using bundled screening perks
competitors.primary_value_prop: Great Eastern's wellness rewards programme with OCBC Premier Banking integration.
strengths: OCBC Premier Banking cross-sell channel (HNW-adjacent)
Long-standing brand trust in SG/MY
Partners with Parkway and Raffles for redemption
weaknesses: Same insurance constraints — no WB-MRI
HNW segment treats benefit as bonus not anchor
No direct threat to AEVUM positioning

competitors.name: MOH Polyclinic + Screen for Life (SG)
competitors.target_market: SG citizens/PRs aged 40+ eligible for subsidised screening + age-appropriate tests
competitors.primary_value_prop: National baseline screening programme available at all polyclinics and GP partners for nominal cost.
strengths: Near-universal access for SG citizens/PRs
Effectively free at point of use
Trusted government delivery channel
weaknesses: Includes no imaging — basic labs and BP only
Slots are often 6–12 months out
Entirely disjoint from HNW segment's needs

competitors.name: Mount Alvernia LTC Screening (SG)
competitors.target_market: SG cost-conscious middle-class families seeking affordable not-for-profit screening
competitors.primary_value_prop: Not-for-profit Catholic hospital offering screening at cost-plus pricing with genuine medical rigour.
strengths: Lowest SG hospital prices for executive-tier screening
Strong community-hospital reputation
Mission-driven brand; high trust
weaknesses: Does not offer whole-body MRI package
No concierge / lifestyle framing
Not targeted at HNW segment
